---
name: Reliable YouTube channels for jazz recordings
description: List of YouTube channels that are reliable sources for matching jazz recordings to videos
type: reference
---

## Most Reliable (Official/Auto-generated)

- **"Artist - Topic" channels**: Auto-generated by YouTube from streaming services. These are the most reliable because the metadata comes directly from the distributor. Examples: "Paul Desmond - Topic", "Jimmy Forrest - Topic", "Scott Hamilton - Topic".
- **Official artist channels**: E.g., "Nat King Cole" (Virgin Music Group), "The Platters" (UMG). Check the description for "Provided to YouTube by [label]" to confirm legitimacy.

## Reliable Archives

- **The78Prof**: Reputable 78rpm archive channel. Provides accurate metadata including catalog numbers (e.g., "Victor 26346"). Good for pre-1950s recordings.
- **Overjazz**: Jazz music upload channel with good metadata.
- **Okmusix**: Music archive channel, generally accurate titles.
- **BlueNote 33RPM**: LP rip channel with good jazz content.

## Identification Tips

- Topic channel videos have descriptions starting with "Provided to YouTube by [distributor]" followed by structured metadata (song, artist, album, label, release year).
- Official channels often have the same "Provided to YouTube by" format.
- For 78rpm/vinyl rip channels, look for catalog numbers in the title/description to cross-reference with MusicBrainz release data.
- User uploads from channels like "soulbrothanumbahone" or "johnnystaccata" are often accurate but harder to verify programmatically.
